Explanation: CAG submits reports to the President.

Subject: Polity
Topic: General
Question Text: In India, the right to vote is a:
Explanation: The right to vote in India is a legal right, conferred by the Representation of the People Act, 1951, though it is rooted in the constitutional principle of universal adult suffrage (Article 326).

Subject: Polity
Topic: Fundamental Rights
Question Text: A legislation which confers on the executive or administrative authority an unguided and uncontrolled discretionary power in the matter of application of laws violates which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India?
Explanation: Article 14 guarantees equality before the law and equal protection of the laws. A legislation granting unguided discretionary power violates this principle by allowing arbitrary application of laws.

Subject: Polity
Topic: Constitutional Amendments
Question Text: The object of the 98th Amendment to the Constitution of India is to
Option A: provide reservation to the socially and educationally backward classes

Correct Answer Text: provide reservation to the socially and educationally backward classes
Explanation: The 98th Amendment to the Constitution of India pertains to providing reservation to the socially and educationally backward classes.

Subject: Polity
Topic: Historical Background (Regulating Act 1773 to Independence Act 1947)

Topic: Making of the Constitution & Salient Features

Explanation: Economic justice is mentioned in the Preamble and DPSP.

Subject: Polity

Explanation: Statement 1 is correct. A private member's bill is indeed introduced by a member of parliament who is not a minister. Statement 2 is incorrect. Several private member's bills have been passed in the past, though it is rare.

Correct Answer Text: Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister
Explanation: The Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister signifies real executive power.

Explanation: The Preamble embodies liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith, and worship, and secures social, economic, and political justice.

Explanation: Judicial activism enforces accountability but can also lead to overreach.

Subject: Polity

Subject: Polity
Topic: High Courts & Subordinate Courts
Question Text:
Which one of the following statements is correct?
(a) In India, the High Courts have the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights only.
(b) In India, the High Courts have the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights as well as for any other purpose.
(c) The Supreme Court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights only.
(d) The Supreme Court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights as well as for any other purpose.
Option A: In India, the High Courts have the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights only.
Option B: In India, the High Courts have the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights as well as for any other purpose.
Option C: The Supreme Court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights only.
Option D: The Supreme Court has the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights as well as for any other purpose.
Correct Answer Text: In India, the High Courts have the power to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights as well as for any other purpose.
Explanation: High Courts can issue writs for fundamental rights and other purposes; Supreme Court only for fundamental rights.

Subject: Polity
Topic: High Courts & Subordinate Courts
Question Text: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the establishment of a common High Court for two or more states?
Option A: It can be established by an Act of Parliament.
Option B: It can be established by a resolution passed by the concerned State Legislatures.
Option C: It can be established by an order of the President of India.
Option D: It can be established by the Supreme Court.
Correct Answer Text: It can be established by an Act of Parliament.
Explanation: A common High Court can be established by an Act of Parliament.

Subject: Polity

Subject: Polity
Topic: General
Question Text: What will follow if a Money Bill is substantially amended by the Rajya Sabha?
Option A: The Lok Sabha may still proceed with the Bill, accepting or not accepting the recommendations of the Rajya Sabha.
Option B: The Lok Sabha cannot consider the Bill further.
Option C: The Lok Sabha may send the Bill to the Rajya Sabha for reconsideration.
Option D: The President may call a joint sitting for passing the Bill.
Correct Answer Text: The Lok Sabha may still proceed with the Bill, accepting or not accepting the recommendations of the Rajya Sabha.
Explanation: The Lok Sabha has the final say on a Money Bill and can choose to accept or reject any or all of the recommendations made by the Rajya Sabha.
